title: ThermoClinics | Measure your core temperature while running
meta_description: During a ThermoClinic you measure your own core temperature on a training run. Learn to recognise, prevent and treat heat illness. An initiative supported by the Heat-Aware Sports Foundation.
hero_eyebrow: Live core temperature measurement during your run
hero_title: Know how hot you really run
hero_lead: During a ThermoClinic you measure your own core temperature on a training run. You see live what exertion does to your body, and you learn how to recognise, treat and prevent heat illness.
hero_note: Max 15 participants per clinic. Also on location for running groups and clubs.
chart_fig_aria: Example chart of core temperature during a training run
chart_svg_aria: Chart: core temperature rises from 37 to just over 39 degrees and keeps climbing for a while after the finish. Above 40 degrees the risk of heatstroke rises sharply.
chart_caption: Your temperature lags behind your effort: it often keeps rising even after the finish. You'll see this on your own data during the clinic.
stat1_label: core temperature above which the risk of heatstroke rises sharply
stat2_label: run at your own pace, with a CORE sensor
stat4_label: per participant, group rate on request
clinic_title: From theory to your own data in three steps
clinic_intro: Not a dry presentation, but hands-on experience. You learn how thermoregulation works, measure your core temperature on a real run, and then analyse your own results.
step1_text: How does your body regulate its temperature? Why do you perform worse in the heat? You learn to recognise, treat and prevent heat illness, with insights from research and elite sport.
step2_text: You're given a CORE sensor and a sports watch for a run at your own pace. You see live how quickly your core temperature climbs towards 39°C.
step3_text: We look at your temperature curve together. You see how your temperature lags behind your heart rate and even keeps rising after the run. That's how you translate the theory to your own races.
photo1_alt: Participants with CORE sensor during the run
learn_title: Perform more safely and better in the heat
learn1_text: What happens in your body during exertion and heat? Knowledge from research, including the Physiology department at Radboudumc.
learn2_text: Practical techniques from elite sport to start cooler, stay cooler and recover faster.
learn3_text: The signs of heat exhaustion and heatstroke, in yourself and in others, and what to do immediately.
learn4_text: Match your pace, ambition and drinking to the conditions. Heat illness can occur from a feels-like temperature of just 15°C.
who_title: For anyone who runs or coaches runners
who_intro: Heatstroke often strikes precisely the fit, well-trained and motivated athletes. Awareness matters for every runner.
who1_text: Sign up for a scheduled clinic. Ideal preparation for summer races such as the Dam tot Damloop or an autumn marathon.
who2_text: From five participants we come to your club. Location, date and time by arrangement, including weekdays during the day or in the evening.
who3_text: Prepare your participants for hot editions and strengthen your safety policy. See also hittebewustsporten.nl for advice for organisations.
agenda_intro: The dates come live from our calendar. Don't see your date? Email us and we'll plan one by arrangement, including weekdays during the day or in the evening.
agenda_note: Maximum 15 participants per clinic. Once full, it's full.
why_p1: In 2014 Arthur Fonville, 24, died of heatstroke at the finish of the Dam tot Damloop. His father Joost then started ThermoClinics with one goal: to prevent others from going through this.
why_p2: In 2025 Joost founded the Heat-Aware Sports Foundation (Stichting Hittebewust Sporten). Its goal: to make athletes, organisers and healthcare professionals aware of the risks of exercising in the heat. Every year in the Netherlands, athletes end up in hospital with heat illness, while with good preparation this is almost always preventable.
fact1_text: During heatstroke the central nervous system becomes disrupted: confusion, slurred speech, neurological symptoms. Vital organs sustain damage.
fact2_text: Because of the intensity and duration of the effort, heat illness can already develop from a feels-like temperature of 15°C.
fact3_text: Contrary to common belief, heatstroke doesn't mainly strike untrained or older athletes. The typical victim is young, fit and motivated.
quote_text: “I knew hot weather was tough. But only when I watched my own core temperature climb towards 39 degrees did I really get it.”
quote_submit: Done a clinic yourself? Share your experience
prak_item2_value: From 5 participants, your own clinic at a group rate
prak_item4_value: 5 to 10 km, at your own pace
prak_item5_value: CORE sensor and sports watch provided on loan
prak_item6_value: Weekdays during the day or in the evening also possible
signup_text: Don't see your preferred date, or coming as a group? Email to info@thermoclinics.nl and we'll arrange a location and time together.
footer_tagline: During a ThermoClinic, you experience first-hand how your body responds to exertion and heat: you measure your own core temperature and learn to recognize and prevent heat injury. Through this hands-on experience, ThermoClinics contributes to the broader mission of Stichting Hittebewust Sporten: advancing knowledge and awareness to help prevent heat stroke in athletes.
footer_slogan: Exercising in the heat can be safe, if you know what you're doing.
